note: h-AUROC mean +/- std (n=3 seeds). Winner per row bolded in .md version. Baseline is v33 matched-protocol re-pretrain. v30 reference uses per-dataset tuning (different protocol, not directly comparable). BOLD = best among {baseline, ch-drop, ST-JEPA}.

comparison_table.note: ch-drop +0.013 vs baseline, p=0.465 (not significant). ST-JEPA -0.076, p=0.034 (significant regression).

SMAP best ch-drop rate is 0.0 -- no dropout. Apparent +0.044 is re-run luck under high baseline variance (std=0.097). ST-JEPA -0.043, p=0.520 (not significant but collapses to near-chance). Both below v30 reference.

ch-drop +0.011 vs baseline, p=0.803 (not significant). ST-JEPA -0.227, p=0.020 (significant regression). Baseline already 0.065 below v30 reference (protocol mismatch). ST-JEPA absolute 0.494 is near chance for FD001.

summary.overall_verdict: No significant improvement from either channel-dropout or ST-JEPA. ST-JEPA collapses on all three datasets (p<0.05 regression on PSM and FD001). Channel-dropout effects are within seed noise on all datasets.
